draft.introduction: Utterance 264 is a spell for the king’s celestial ascent, describing him as a wild bull of the sky who devours the gods’ power. It is part of the broader ascension corpus within the Pyramid Texts.

translation.text: The King is the wild bull of the sky, whose heart desires what comes of himself. The King’s mother, the great wild cow, who dwells on the Mountain of the Sunrise—she will nurse the King and will not wean him.
translation.original_text: kā n(j) p.t pw nsw.t ḫā.t(j)-jb(.j) m pr.t m=f. mw.t nsw.t ḥsā.t wr.t ḥr ḏw bāḫw

translator_notes.note: The metaphor of the king as a “wild bull of the sky” connects royal power to bovine symbolism that pervades Egyptian religion (Apis, Hathor, Buchis). The nursing mother who “will not wean him” promises eternal sustenance—the dead king receives divine milk forever.
